# FIX-001: Detail Pages 400 Error — undefined UUID in Params

## Problem

All transaction list pages (Request, Loan, Return, Handover, Project) navigate to detail pages using `.uuid` property which does not exist on the response types. The actual field is `.id`.

**Backend logs:**

```
GET /api/v1/requests/undefined 400 - "Validation failed (uuid is expected)"
GET /api/v1/loans/undefined 400 - "Validation failed (uuid is expected)"
GET /api/v1/returns/undefined 400 - "Validation failed (uuid is expected)"
GET /api/v1/projects/PRJ-2026-0410-0001 400 - "Validation failed (uuid is expected)"
```

## Root Cause

Frontend list pages accessed `.uuid` on API response objects, but the TypeScript types and API responses use `.id` as the UUID field name.

## Fix

Changed `.uuid` → `.id` in navigate calls across 5 list pages:

| File                   | Old                                    | New                                  |
| ---------------------- | -------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------ |
| `RequestListPage.tsx`  | `navigate(\`/requests/${req.uuid}\`)`  | `navigate(\`/requests/${req.id}\`)`  |
| `LoanListPage.tsx`     | `navigate(\`/loans/${loan.uuid}\`)`    | `navigate(\`/loans/${loan.id}\`)`    |
| `ReturnListPage.tsx`   | `navigate(\`/returns/${ret.uuid}\`)`   | `navigate(\`/returns/${ret.id}\`)`   |
| `HandoverListPage.tsx` | `navigate(\`/handovers/${ho.uuid}\`)`  | `navigate(\`/handovers/${ho.id}\`)`  |
| `ProjectListPage.tsx`  | `navigate(\`/projects/${proj.uuid}\`)` | `navigate(\`/projects/${proj.id}\`)` |

Note: `RepairListPage.tsx` already used `.id` correctly.

## Date

2026-04-12
